entities.name: PARTICIPATORY SCENARIO PROCESSES
entities.description: A participatory process used to catalyze individual and collective transformation and policy change by enhancing systems understanding, learning, and networking.

entities.name: TRANSFORMATIONAL CHANGE
entities.description: Deep systemic change in individual and collective practices and policies.

entities.name: POLICY CHANGE
entities.description: Modifications in policy frameworks to address issues such as food security.

entities.name: SYSTEMS UNDERSTANDING
entities.description: The ability of participants to comprehend the complex interactions within a system.

entities.name: LEARNING
entities.description: The process of acquiring knowledge or skills through participatory exercises.

entities.name: NETWORKING
entities.description: The development of connections and collaborations among participants.

entities.name: PRACTICES
entities.description: The actual behaviors and methods implemented by individuals.

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: The country where the district-level case study on food security and climate change was conducted.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient, affordable, and nutritious food.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns affecting agricultural systems.

entities.name: ADAPTIVE CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of participants to adjust to potential damage or take advantage of opportunities.

entities.name: COLLECTIVE ACTION
entities.description: Action taken together by a group to achieve a common goal.

entities.name: RESILIENCE OF AGRICULTURAL AND FOOD SYSTEMS
entities.description: The capacity of food systems to withstand and recover from climate-related shocks.

entities.name: STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of involving people affected by decisions to support learning and evaluation.

relationships.target: RESILIENCE OF AGRICULTURAL AND FOOD SYSTEMS
